summary: Synthesize a scalar Field1 of hash-based value noise in [-1, 1]: each lattice point is a hash of (coordinate, seed) (tiling- and order-invariant, not a sequential RNG), bilinearly blended with a quintic fade.

params.name: width
params.doc: The output field width in pixels.

params.name: height
params.doc: The output field height in pixels.

params.name: frequency
params.doc: Lattice cells per 64 pixels (the base spatial frequency); strictly positive.

params.name: seed
params.doc: The 64-bit seed; the noise is a hash of (coordinate, seed), not a sequential RNG.

perceptual.reason: field.noise is hash-based value noise verified by analytic properties (bit-exact reruns, tiling/evaluation-order invariance via hash-of-coordinate, seed sensitivity, [-1, 1] range); there is no perceptual-quality metric to apply
